excerpt: The Dresden Historians' Commission reported that up to 25,000 people were killed in the air raids on Dresden from 13 to 15 February 1945.
transform_note: Used as the best-supported ceiling and preferred best estimate in the current bundle.
confidence_note: High confidence as the official commission's principal finding.

excerpt: The commission report notes a wartime figure of 22,096 recovered bodies by 3 April 1945.
transform_note: Used as a conservative floor for the direct-death estimate.
confidence_note: Moderate-to-high confidence as an administrative recovered-body tally, but it is not identical to total deaths.
